question_text: Two identical containers are filled with the same volume of different liquids. Container X contains oil (density = 920 kg/m³) and container Y contains brine solution (density = 1100 kg/m³). Which statement correctly compares the masses of the liquids in the two containers?

options.label: A
options.text: Both containers have the same mass because they have the same volume

options.label: B
options.text: Container X has greater mass because oil is less dense

options.label: C
options.text: Container Y has greater mass because the brine solution is more dense

options.label: D
options.text: The mass cannot be determined without knowing the container dimensions

correct_answer: Container Y has greater mass because the brine solution is more dense
explanation: Since density = mass ÷ volume, we can rearrange this to mass = density × volume. Both containers have the same volume, so the container with the higher density will have the greater mass. Brine solution (1100 kg/m³) is denser than oil (920 kg/m³), therefore container Y has greater mass.
